rationale: The cited 2010 paper appears methodologically central: it introduces item randomized response with Bayesian IRT, models instruction noncompliance, and targets social-desirability bias in sensitive marketing surveys—core elements that closely overlap with the 2019 paper’s ICRT contribution (privacy-protected sensitive-behavior measurement, Bayesian IRT, and procedural nonadherence handling). Given this strong conceptual/method lineage and the confirmed DOI-level match in the references, this is not an obvious incidental mention.
